title: Abildgaardia johnstonii
taxonomy: Abildgaardia johnstonii is a species of sedge, classified within the family Cyperaceae. This family includes a diverse range of plants commonly known as sedges, rushes, and clubsedges. As a member of the Cyperaceae family, Abildgaardia johnstonii shares characteristics with other sedges, such as narrow leaves and a distinctive inflorescence. The genus Abildgaardia is further classified within the subfamily Cyperoideae, which is one of the main subfamilies within the Cyperaceae family. The classification of Abildgaardia johnstonii is still subject to ongoing research and updates in the field of botany.

intro: Abildgaardia johnstonii, also known as Johnston's club-rush, is a species of flowering plant in the sedge family (Cyperaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and its anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Abildgaardia johnstonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often prepared as a tea or decoction, and is also used in the form of capsules or tinctures in modern herbal medicine.
